description: Within-set Kalos regional-bird evolution-pair 1:1 mirror. Fletchling DAA-151 (Pokedex no. 661, Normal/Flying base) and Fletchinder DAA-031 (no. 662, Fire/Flying Stage-1) are the Kalos Gen-VI fast-bird line's base and Stage-1, both trivia-passed, both unwired prior to this pass, both in the same set (Darkness Ablaze) at non-adjacent collector numbers. Notable lore detail: this is one of the franchise's few evolution lines where the secondary type CHANGES at first evolution (Normal->Fire), not the usual base-Normal-stays-Normal pattern. The line's apex Talonflame (no. 663, the Gen-VI OU-tier terror via unconditional Gale Wings priority Brave Bird) is not currently in corpus. Line-cohort node deferred at n=2 below the 3+ prints / 2+ stages threshold established by [[mareep-line]] / [[caterpie-line]] / [[staraptor-line]] — this mirror follows the Larvitar/Pupitar precedent of the within-set evolution-pair mirror under threshold. Two card-endpoints, both equal nodes, must be traversable from either side. Hence the mirror. Sigh is professional.
citations: [Bulbapedia: Talonflame — Gen VI OU-tier via Gale Wings]
[Pokemon X and Y (Game Freak, 2013) primary canon for Kalos regional-bird line]
other_candidates_considered: fletchling-line character node — n=2 is below the 3+ prints / 2+ stages threshold; deferred until a Talonflame print enters corpus or a second Fletchling/Fletchinder print appears. Staraptor-line caveats already flag this as 'may be near-threshold' — verifying that flag turned out to be at exactly threshold-minus-one.

Fletchling attached to staraptor-line as regional-bird-line sibling — rejected, cross-line edges between different regional-bird-lines are a tag-bridge not a graph edge; future regional-bird-lines parent node is the right anchor when 3+ sibling regional-bird-line nodes exist.
